downgradedClaims.claim: Protocol-first risk is systematic across every benchmark.
downgradedClaims.evidence: Letter and control-style targets show low or absent protocol effects.

downgradedClaims.claim: metric_stress_validator discovers split risk by itself.
downgradedClaims.evidence: It packages controls and class-risk evidence but does not replace model-family and split-family experiments.

downgradedClaims.claim: Source-described files equal official leaderboard reproduction.
downgradedClaims.evidence: The program followed source files but did not reproduce external leaderboard protocols.

preservedClaims.claim: Protocol-first evaluation changes conclusions on Shuttle.
Landsat remains a different spatial/file protocol-risk case.

claimBindings.claim: Shuttle is the strongest rare-class metric-risk target in this program.
Landsat is a distinct spatial/file protocol-risk target.
Protocol ambiguity matters even when metric delta is small.
The split-risk thesis is not universal.
Protocol-card replay should be promoted as a support tool.

har.name: UCI Human Activity Recognition Using Smartphones

optical.name: UCI Optical Recognition of Handwritten Digits

pen.name: UCI Pen-Based Recognition of Handwritten Digits

rejectedAlternatives: More random UCI scoring was rejected because it would not test protocol claims.
Repo/test reproduction was deferred because it is a different research family.
Time-series anomaly negative controls were deferred until protocol-card replay is better established.
nextProgram.stopCriteria: Stop or narrow if fewer than three targets have reproducible source protocols or if low-risk controls dominate the results.
nextProgram.title: Protocol-Card Replay at Scale
weeks: Week 1: build protocol cards for ten candidate benchmarks and run three source-vs-random comparisons.
Week 2: add replay and model-family stress on five protocol-bearing targets.
Week 3: run one deep ambiguous-protocol target with explicit downgrade criteria.
Week 4: kill week and next-target selection.
nextProgram.whySelected: It is the most direct next step from the evidence: protocol extraction, source-vs-random comparisons, negative controls, and replay can be scaled without adding product framework layers.
